body:
Yep, exactly the same thing happened to me just now.

EDIT: Interesting, copying or symlinking the starship.exe located in `C:\Program Files\starship\bin` to `C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship\tools` (had to create the "tools" directory beforehand) results in the same error but with a different path:

`C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship\lib\starship\tools\starship.exe`

The problem is probably somehow connected to Choco's [executable shimming](https://docs.chocolatey.org/en-us/features/shim) but I can't know for sure.

EDIT 2: I used the wrong target before... Even though I wrote `C:\Program Files\starship\bin` I was actually using the shimmed executable located in `C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\bin`. So, creating the "tools" directory under `C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship` and then symlinking the correct starship.exe works:

`mklink "C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship\tools\starship.exe" "C:\Program Files\starship\bin\starship.exe"`

It's a workaround but also a pretty ugly hack. :grimacing:

Uninstalling `starship` and switching to `starship.install` or `starship.portable` should also work. Please also consider switching to winget.
This sounds like a bug in chocolatey 2.0 please try to report the bug there as well.

Thank you for the response.

`choco install starship.install` correctly installed `1.14.2`

After uninstalling, I tried the "portable" version.
`choco install starship.portable` correctly installed `1.15.0`

I had `winget` on my radar for a while so it'll check it out.

Thanks again for the response. Closing issue as resolved.

> This sounds like a bug in chocolatey 2.0 please try to report the bug there as well.

As we pointed out over on the [GitHub issue](https://github.com/chocolatey/choco/issues/3204#issuecomment-1582186233) we can't reproduce the issue and [neither can the user](https://github.com/chocolatey/choco/issues/3204#issuecomment-1582179595). 

> Please also consider switching to winget. 

What does the WinGet manifest do that the Chocolatey package doesn't?

> `choco install starship.install` correctly installed `1.14.2`

`choco install starship.install` correctly installed 1.15.0 for me. 

![image](https://github.com/starship/starship/assets/12760779/7e172df1-edbe-40ad-828e-62e263a117cf)

As did `starship.portable`:

![image](https://github.com/starship/starship/assets/12760779/9a43fca5-4fb9-4d0c-acab-e794195f3d35)

If you're getting something different I would suggest it's a local issue and not a Chocolatey package one.

